utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hi , brian . how are you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hi , good to see you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: it's good to see you too . so , i know the nurse told you a little bit about dax .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'd like to tell dax about you , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , brian is a 58 year old male with a past medical history significant for congestive heart failure and hypertension , who presents today for follow-up of his chronic problems . so , brian , it's been a little while i've seen you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: whats , what's going on ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i , i just feel out of sorts lately . i do n't know if it's the change in the seasons or if we're just doing a lot of projects around the house and , and some , some construction on our own . i'm just feeling out of it . lack of , uh , energy . i'm just so tired and fatigued , and i feel kinda ... i feel lightheaded every once in a while .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . um , how long has that been going on for ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , probably since labor day , so about five weeks or so .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and , have you noticed any , like , symptoms of weight gain , like , like swollen legs , or , you know , your belly feels bloated and things like that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i feel , i feel bloated every once in a while .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . um , and , are you taking your , your medications ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , yes , i am .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and , how about your diet ? are you watching your diet ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , it's been a little bit of a struggle . we began construction on our kitchen over labor day weekend , and it was ... hard to cook or prepare meals so we ate out a lot, and not always the best food out. it , it , it kind of reeked havoc , uh , so it's been maybe off a little bit .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . and , how about , you know , other symptoms , like , have you had a fever or chills ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , and any problems breathing ? do you feel short of breath ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , just when i'm doing doing the projects . again , not even lifting anything really heavy , it's just that if i'm ex- exerting any energy , i , i kinda feel it at that point .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . do you have any chest pain ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: slight cramps . that seems to go away after about , maybe about an hour or so after i first feel it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , and how about a cough ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: a , a slight cough , and again , i'm not sure if it's just the change of seasons and i'm getting a cold .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: mm-hmm . okay . all right . well , you know , for the most part , how , you know , before all of this-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: . how were you doing with your heart failure ? i know that we've kinda talked about you being able to watch your healthy food intake and that's been kind of a struggle in the past .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i , i , i've actually been pretty good about that ever since . the , the , the last year , it's been a little chaotic , but i wanted to make sure i stayed on top of that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . are you excited for halloween ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , ca n't wait .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: our home renovations should be complete by then

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right , yeah , right .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and , so , lastly , for your high blood pressure , how are you doing with that ? have , are , did you buy the blood pressure cuff like i asked ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , i , i did , and we do mon- , i , i monitor it regularly . my wife makes sure i stay on top of that , but it's been pretty good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . well , i know you did the review of systems sheet when you checked in , and you were endorsing this fatigue-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: . and a little dizziness and we just talked a lot about a lot of other symptoms .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: any other symptoms i might be missing ? nausea or vomiting , diarrhea ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: anything like that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . well , i just want to go ahead and do a quick physical exam .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon ? show me the vital signs . so , looking at your vital signs here in the office , everything looks good . you know , your blood pressure and your heart rate and your oxygenation all look really good .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , i'm gon na just take a listen to a few things and check some things out , and i'll let you know what i find , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: perfect .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so , on your physical examination , you know , i do appreciate some jugular venous distention to-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: to about eight centimeters . on your heart exam , i do appreciate a three out of six systolic ejection murmur , which we've heard in the past . and , on your lung exam , i do appreciate some fine crackles at the bases bilaterally , and your lower extremities have , you know , 1+ pitting edema . so , what does all that mean ? that means i think you're retaining a little bit of fluid .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay ? i wan na just go ahead and look at some of your results , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon ? show me the chest x-ray . so , looking here at the results of your chest x-ray , it does look like you have a little bit of fluid in your lungs there , and that can be just from , um , your heart failure , okay ? hey , dragon ? show me the echocardiogram . so , this is the echocardiogram that we did about four months ago , and this shows that the pumping function of your heart is a little bit reduced at 45 % , and it also shows that leaky valve , the mitral regurgitation that , that you have , okay ? um , so , let me just go over and talk about , a little bit , my assessment and my plan for you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay ? so , for your first problem , your congestive heart failure , i think you're retaining fluid , and i wan na go ahead and increase your lasix to 80 mg once a day .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i want you to weigh yourself every day . i want you to call me if you're gaining more weight .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and , i certainly want you to call me if you have any other symptoms of shortness of breath , and i wan na go ahead and order another echocardiogram , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon ? order an echocardiogram . lastly , for your high blood pressure , it looks like you're managing it well at this time , okay ? so , i wan na go ahead and continue with the lisinopril 20 mg a day . i want you to continue to record your blood pressures at home , and report them to me in the patient portal if you see they're getting elevated , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: does that sound like a plan ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that sounds fine .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . um , i'm gon na be in touch with you after we get your test results , and we'll go from there , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . hey , dragon , finalize the note .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 58-year-old male presenting for follow-up of congestive heart failure and hypertension.
The doctor asks the patient about his current symptoms and concerns.
The patient reports fatigue, lightheadedness, and lack of energy for the past five weeks.
The doctor asks about the duration of the patient's symptoms.
The patient reports symptoms have been present for approximately five weeks.
The doctor asks about symptoms of weight gain, leg swelling, and bloating.
The patient reports occasional bloating.
The doctor asks if the patient is taking his medications.
The patient confirms he is taking his medications.
The doctor asks about the patient's diet.
The patient reports difficulty maintaining a healthy diet due to home renovations.
The doctor asks if the patient has experienced fevers or chills.
The patient denies fevers or chills.
The doctor asks if the patient is experiencing shortness of breath.
The patient reports shortness of breath upon exertion.
The doctor asks if the patient has chest pain.
The patient reports experiencing slight chest cramps during exertion.
The doctor asks if the patient has a cough.
The patient reports a slight cough.
Congestive heart failure
The patient reports he has been compliant with his diet regarding salt intake.
Hypertension
The doctor asks if the patient is monitoring his blood pressure at home.
The patient confirms he monitors his blood pressure regularly and it has been well controlled.
The doctor asks if the patient has nausea, vomiting, or diarrhea.
The patient denies nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea.
Physical exam findings include JVD of 8 cm, 3/6 systolic ejection murmur, rales at bilateral lung bases, and 1+ pitting edema in lower extremities.
Chest X-ray shows mild pulmonary fluid; echocardiogram shows 45% ejection fraction and mild mitral regurgitation.
Increase Lasix to 80 mg daily.
The patient is instructed to weigh himself daily and report weight gain.
The patient is instructed to report dyspnea and will have a repeat echocardiogram.
Continue lisinopril 20 mg daily.
The patient is instructed to continue home blood pressure monitoring and report elevations via the patient portal.

case_description: follow-up of chronic problems; congestive heart failure;hypertension
